dhp167:4: Don’t busy yourself with the world.
dhp168:3: One who lives the Dhamma sleeps with ease
dhp169:3: One who lives the Dhamma sleeps with ease
dhp170:3: one who regards the world this way
dhp170:4: the King of Death doesn’t see.
dhp171:2: all decked out like a royal chariot,
dhp171:4: while those who know don’t cling.
dhp172:4: like the moon set free from a cloud.
dhp173:4: like the moon set free from a cloud.
dhp174:3: Just as birds who’ve escaped from a net are
dhp174:4: few, few are the people who make it to heaven.
dhp175:1: Swans fly the path of the sun;
dhp175:2: those with the power fly through space;
dhp175:3: the enlightened flee from the world,
dhp175:4: having defeated the armies of Mara.
dhp176:0: The person who tells a lie,
dhp176:1: who transgresses in this one thing,
dhp176:3: transcending concern for the world beyond:
dhp176:4: there’s no evil he might not do.
dhp177:1: No misers go to the world of the devas.
dhp177:2: Those who don’t praise giving are fools.
dhp177:3: The enlightened express their approval for giving
dhp177:4: and so find ease in the world beyond.
dhp178:4: the fruit of stream entry excels them.
